mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate signals easier financial conditions and lower discount rates, which lifts equity valuations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when inflation is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in at least 55% of months with a 3-month FEDFUNDS decline, or if the signal loses sign after controlling for CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent level marks labor market deterioration, weakening household income expectations and corporate sales, which depresses S&P 500 returns over the following month. The signal is activated by a clear monthly deterioration rather than the level alone.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of cases, or if PAYEMS growth stays positive and the signal still predicts losses.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized rebound in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broad domestic demand strengthening, which supports revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect requires both retail spending and housing activity to improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if joint upturns in RSAFS and HOUST are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns more than half the time, or if either series alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in INDPRO reflects manufacturing and industrial output acceleration, which signals improving real activity and supports cyclical earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week. The signal should work when output growth is broad rather than inventory-driven.
failure_condition: Reject if weeks with INDPRO growth above the threshold do not have positive 5-day S&P 500 returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears after excluding months with flat PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: A low and falling FEDFUNDS rate combined with stable CPIAUCSL inflation creates a favorable real-rate backdrop, compressing equity discount rates and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when inflation rises at the same time as easing.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate/low-inflation condition does not yield positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of observations, or if returns are not better than using FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A broad PAYEMS decline accompanied by a rising UNRATE signals labor demand contraction, reducing earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The mechanism is strongest when payroll losses persist for several months.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not negative in at least 60% of payroll-decline episodes, or if UNRATE does not rise alongside PAYEMS losses.

mechanism_narrative: An upward inflection in GDPC1 indicates real output growth is reaccelerating, which improves revenue outlook and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when GDP surprises are not offset by weak retail sales.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth accelerates versus prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if GDP acceleration periods do not produce positive 20-day returns more often than baseline, or if the effect vanishes when RSAFS is weak.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, weak INDPRO combined with elevated FEDFUNDS signals policy pressure is restraining real activity, which should push S&P 500 returns lower over the next week. The mechanism depends on both restrictive rates and slowing industrial production together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/weak-output condition fails to generate negative 5-day returns in at least 55% of cases, or if INDPRO alone explains the effect without FEDFUNDS.
